verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: No permanent takeover possible; Auto-release on failure; Invariant tested; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.ttl_evidence: Every lease is time-bounded: an expired lease auto-releases on sweep with a ttl-expire audit entry, leaving zero holds — nothing can be held forever.
gate_result.failure_evidence: A failing acquire (missing GPU node) leaves zero holds after the auto-release path.
gate_result.invariant_evidence: The little-brother-always-functions invariant (I13) is tested live: DURING a LifeOS hold the host still spawns processes, reads the filesystem, and binds other ports normally.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: Real TTL expiry sweep, real failure path, real host operations during a live hold.
gate_result.red_first: gates committed red with the control plane absent (8/8 files failing) at the lane red commit, green at HEAD

proof_summary: ARCHBP-106 is complete: no permanent takeover is possible — controls are TTL-bounded with audited auto-release on expiry and on failure, and the little-brother-always-functions invariant is tested live with the host fully functional during a LifeOS hold.
